mission.name: Artemis II
mission.status: TRANSLUNAR COAST — MET +2d 03:25 — Integrity ~165,000 mi from Earth, outbound. FD4: crew studying lunar geography targets for FD6 flyby. KPAE 13°C CLR WSW 240° — clear evening. BEE research mission EXECUTED: 9 pages live on tibsfox.com/Research/BEE/. Session 5 total: 24+ new pages, 20 research summaries, GSD-2 v2.59.0. 20/24 Day 4 hours.

space_weather.aurora_risk: none — Kp quiet (1.33). R1 minor radio blackout in effect (HF degradation, sunlit side only). No geomagnetic storm watches. Excellent translunar conditions.
space_weather.r1_impact: Weak HF radio degradation on sunlit side, occasional loss of radio contact

leaders.temp_accuracy: v1.3.14.2: Model predicted 14.2°C, actual 12.0°C — 2.2°C overshoot. Afternoon cooling faster than expected. KSEA 13°C tracks closer.
leaders.wind_accuracy: v1.3.14.2: MAJOR MISS — model predicted NW 8 km/h (320°), actual SE 5.5 km/h (140°). 180° direction error. Onshore marine flow arrived early, not offshore ridge flow.
leaders.overall: 28 samples. Day 4 afternoon: model humidity miss +21% (predicted 33%, actual 54%). Marine layer infiltrating — spread collapsed to 9°C. Clouds lower (SCT035 BKN090 vs predicted SCT070). Ridge weaker than modeled. KSEA OVC065 confirms marine push. KSC 26.8°C FEW034 ESE 24 km/h — warmer winds at Cape.

mission_timeline.flight_day_3: ACTIVE — TRAJECTORY CORRECTION #1 CANCELLED — spacecraft already on correct lunar flyby path after TLI precision. No burn needed. Crew woke from 8hr rest at 2:17 PM EDT, began FD3 ops. CPR demo in microgravity complete. Medical kit checkout complete (thermometer, BP monitor, stethoscope, otoscope). Koch tests Orion emergency comms on DSN. Full crew rehearses FD6 lunar flyby observation choreography. Source: blogs.nasa.gov/artemis FD3 updates.
mission_timeline.flight_day_4: Trajectory correction #2 refines lunar path. Each astronaut: 1hr reviewing geographic targets for FD6 lunar photography. 20min dedicated celestial body photography from Orion windows. Source: nasa.gov/missions/artemis daily agenda.
mission_timeline.flight_day_5: Enter lunar sphere of influence. Spacesuit testing (Orion OCSS donning, pressurization, helmet port eating). Final outbound burn.
mission_timeline.flight_day_6: CLOSEST APPROACH — 4,000-6,000 mi from lunar surface. Record distance from Earth (~252,021 mi, beating Apollo 13). Photos/video all day. 30-50 min comm blackout behind Moon.
mission_timeline.flight_day_7: Exit lunar sphere. First return trajectory correction. Off-duty recovery day.
mission_timeline.flight_day_8: Radiation shelter demo (solar flare protection). Manual piloting test (6-DOF vs 3-DOF control).
mission_timeline.flight_day_9: Reentry procedure review. Return trajectory correction #2. Waste system test. Orthostatic garment fit.
mission_timeline.flight_day_10: Final trajectory correction. Cabin reconfig. SM separation. Direct entry at 11.0 km/s, 3000°F. Drogue→pilot→main chutes. Splashdown Pacific, ~17 mph. April 10, 8:06 PM EDT.
mission_timeline.pcd_issue: Koch PCD went dark before TLI, unresponsive. Mission control working alternatives. Non-critical — backup procedures available.
mission_timeline.tdrs_issue: Brief loss of two-way comms post-orbit insertion. Traced to ground TDRS configuration. Resolved, no mission impact.

announcements.text: v1.3.16: MET+2d00:25 4pm PDT. Hourly sweep. Session 5 continuation: DRP Cascadia page (geology, scenario, ShakeAlert, preparedness). Research #37 (trust-based fintech, Nigeria) + #38 (cognitive load, AI). OPEN #19 on index. AREDN URL fix. 10+ fact-checks cleared. Radio guide linked. Approaching 2-day MET mark. 150K mi outbound.

v1.3.15: MET+1d23:25 3pm PDT. Hourly sweep. Session 5 productive: CERN ALICE + LHCb detail pages (4/4 experiments complete, all cross-linked). CSP Pathway 2 Web & Networking. Space weather feed page (LIVE). MUK stats fixed (28 samples, 4 days). Julia 1.12.5 installed. KPAE 13.8°C NW 6 SCT060, afternoon cooling. TC#1 cancelled, path correct. Integrity ~145K mi outbound.

v1.3.14.2: MET+1d22:55 2:30pm PDT. Session 5 begins. TRAJECTORY CORRECTION #1 CANCELLED — Orion already on correct lunar flyby path, no burn needed (nasa.gov/blogs/artemis). Live METAR: KPAE 12°C SE 5.5 SCT035 BKN090 (model miss: predicted NW 8 FEW080 14.2°C — 180° wind error, marine push arrived early). R1 minor radio blackout active. KSEA 13°C OVC065 W 11. KSC 26.8°C FEW034 ESE 24. Integrity ~140K mi outbound.

v1.3.12: MET+1d21:00 noon PDT. Backfill hours 7-12. FD3 deep-space ops: crew awake from 8hr rest, exercise tested, CPR demo complete, trajectory correction #1 imminent. KPAE warming to 14.0°C NW 6 FEW080, high pressure ridge. Session 4: 10/11 research videos processed, crew-life page, CWC NASA food standards, Fox Bar concept, www-tibsfox repo (5,173 files). Integrity ~120K mi outbound.

v1.3.2: MET+1d10:50 2am PDT. Session 4 begins. Sweep cadence resumed — Day 4 weather data added, regex patterns fixed for v1.2+. FD3 underway: trajectory correction #1, CPR demo, DSN test. KPAE 5.0°C calm CLR, high pressure ridge building. Integrity ~80K mi outbound.

v1.2.24: MET+1d10:40 2am PDT. Session 3 final. 32 commits. 28 new pages: CERN (2), DRP (2), MUS (16 — index, 5 pathways, 10 nodes), CSP (2 — index, languages), AI coverage, 2 crew bios, RPI lessons. 19 fact-check corrections. 38 videos + 31 channels queued for research (11.3MB transcripts). Slime mold sim deployed. 4 papers. Integrity ~75K mi outbound, FD3 tomorrow.

v1.2.22: MET+1d06:30 10pm PDT. Session 3 wrap sweep. 15 commits this session. New content: CERN root page + WLCG deep dive, DRP disaster recovery + zero-trust comms, AI coverage analysis (Anthropic replication), Physarum slime mold sim, crew-douglas + crew-gibbons backup bios, 5 music theory knowledge nodes, papers #31-34. KPAE 7.0C calm CLR, clear night. FD3 tomorrow.

v1.2.21: MET+1d05:30 9pm PDT. Translunar coast night watch. Papers #31-34 cataloged (Kuramoto 50yr, proportional coupling, HERA, Typed CoT). AI coverage analysis page published. Physarum slime mold network sim deployed to forest ecosystem. KPAE 7.5C calm CLR, cooling into clear night. FD3 tomorrow: trajectory correction #1.

v1.2.20: MET+1d05:00 8pm PDT. Translunar coast steady state. Backfilled sweeps 19-20. 5 music theory knowledge nodes added: subtractive synthesis, patch routing as file tree, oscillator layering, condensation. KPAE 8.0C calm CLR post-frontal clearing. Crew resting. No trajectory corrections reported.

v1.2.10: MET+18:55 10:30am PDT. PRM #2 complete (43s burn, SM main engine, 7:06 AM EDT wake-up to 'Sleepyhead' by Young and Sick). Crew resting 4.5h. TLI at 7:49 PM EDT (4:49 PM PDT): 5m51s burn, 1272 ft/s delta-v. Mission management GO/NO-GO meeting pending. M4.6 Boulder Creek earthquake at 01:41 AM on Sam's territory. USGS Observatory + PNW Daily news page launched.
BACKFILL MET+15:31 7:06am EDT: Crew wake-up. Song: 'Sleepyhead' by Young and Sick. PRM #2 prep begins. Crew checks spacecraft systems.
BACKFILL MET+15:55: PRM #2 COMPLETE. Service module main engine fired 43 seconds. Perigee raised, orbit stabilized for TLI approach. Crew returns to rest for 4.5 hours.

BACKFILL: M4.6 EARTHQUAKE Boulder Creek, CA (nc75337442). 1 km ESE Boulder Creek, depth 10.85 km. 25,658 felt reports. San Andreas fault, strike-slip + reverse. Largest event on Loma Prieta segment since 1989 M6.9. Sam's territory — Kings Creek Road.

BACKFILL MET+06:25: Crew 4-hour nap period. Prox ops complete. Toilet fixed. ICPS deorbited Pacific. Integrity solo in 115 x 43,760 mi orbit. KPAE overnight rain — 0.97 inches April 1, continuing into April 2.

BACKFILL MET+04:25 8pm PDT: Prox ops demo COMPLETE (70 min). Glover manually piloted Integrity within 30 ft of ICPS. Docking camera nav data gathered. ICPS disposal burn set. Press conference: Administrator Isaacman — crew 'safe, secure, in great spirits.' Toilet fault light resolved.

v1.1.17.4: L+2h48m 6:23pm. ARB COMPLETE — 18-min RL10 burn, apogee now 43,760 mi (70,426 km). Highest humans since Apollo 17 Dec 1972. ICPS sep imminent, 70-min prox ops demo: automated backflip, halt at 300ft, manual approach to 30ft, fine handling with RCS thrusters, then automated departure. Toilet fault light reported — under investigation. ICPS will deorbit to Pacific after sep. KPAE 10.8°C RA OVC020. Erdős #19 updated: EFL proved large n, GPU gap = small n verification.

v1.1.16: L+45m 4pm. IN ORBIT. Parking orbit ~267×2005 km, period 90 min. Two orbits then TLI burn at ~L+3h22m. Math Sections 8-10 published: live telemetry orbital mechanics, O2O Shannon link budget, circadian beat frequency. KPAE rain arrived (-RA OVC025, S 15 km/h, 1000.0 hPa). PNW weather prediction confirmed.

v1.1.15.5: IN ORBIT. MECO at T+8:00, core stage sep at T+8:27. All 4 RS-25s nominal through cutoff. ICPS RL-10 ready for orbit insertion burn. Two orbits, then TLI at ~T+3h22m. Wiseman, Glover, Koch, Hansen — first humans in lunar transfer orbit since December 1972. The space between the Moon and the Earth.

v1.1.14.6: T-34m 2:50pm. TERMINAL COUNT APPROACHING. Launch team working LAS battery temperature out of range — must resolve before terminal count pickup. FTS issue from earlier resolved using legacy Shuttle-era equipment. Arm retracted, pad clear. Sound suppression armed. 34 min to window open. KPAE OVC035 S 16. KSC 80% wx GO.

v1.1.13.2: T-104m 1:40pm. HATCH SEAL CHECK PASSED. Suit leak checks passed for all 4 crew. No technical issues — team not working any problems. Cabin pressure integrity confirmed. SLS replenish mode nominal. Weather 80% GO (cumulus cloud rule, precip rule, ground winds — all favorable). Space weather GO. Built-in hold at T-1h10m next major milestone.

v1.1.12: T-3h noon. CREW ONBOARD INTEGRITY. All 4 suits on umbilicals, IVA confirmed. Voice checks complete — CDR Wiseman, PLT Glover, MS1 Koch, MS2 Hansen — Houston confirms. Proc 10.1.1 ingress steps 2-4 done. OE2 confirmed. SLS tanking complete, replenish mode. KSC 28°C clear. KPAE 12.5°C S 12 km/h, overcast building.

v1.1.11: T-4h 11am. SLS fast fill nearly complete. Crew in final medical checks before suit-up. KPAE near daily high 12.0°C, S 10 km/h, BKN040 — marine overcast approaching. Pressure 1001.2 dropping. PNW rain ~5pm, after launch window. KSC 27°C clear, ALL SYSTEMS GO.

v1.1.10: T-5h 10am. SLS tanking slow fill, no H2 leaks. KPAE 11.5°C S 8 km/h, cloud deck lowering BKN050. Onshore flow strengthening — rain forecast evening but launch window clear. KSC 26°C clear GO. Pressure 1001.5 still falling.

v1.1.9: T-6h 9am. SLS TANKING UNDERWAY. LOX/LH2 loading began at T-6h33m. KPAE 10.5°C, S wind developing 5 km/h, scattered clouds building from south. Classic marine push pattern. KSC 24.5°C clear, weather GO. Kp 2.0 steady.
v1.1.8: T-7h 8am. KPAE 9.0°C Calm, SCT060. KSC 23.0°C.

v1.1.7: T-8h 7am LAUNCH DAY. Full content update: 3 new papers (#22-24, cryogenic propellant, post-CME dose, direct entry params). KPAE 7.0°C calm SCT080, solar heating begins. Kp 2.0 recovering. G2 watch holds but improving. 24 papers total. LIVE dashboard updated with GO/NO-GO context. Aurora possible tonight at 55°N.

v1.0.25: T-14h 1am LIVE. KPAE 8.9C gusty S 200@15G18kt, BKN024 OVC033. Rain stopped but low ceiling. Kp back to 1.33 — CME pulse over, good for launch. Spread collapsed to 3.9C. Beth's Cafe deep dive published.
v1.0.24: T-15h midnight. FIRST RAIN! -RA at KPAE. 9.4C, BKN040 OVC065. Dewpoint surging (4.4C). Wind SSE 150@11kt. KSEA wind shift W 240@10kt.
v1.0.23: T-16h 11pm. KPAE cooling 10.6C, S 180@13kt gusting, OVC080. Kp dropping 3.00→1.33 — CME pulse subsiding. KSEA perfect temp match (10.6=10.6).
v1.0.22: T-17h 10pm. KPAE holding 11.1C, wind picking up 170@10kt, overcast 6500ft. KSC 21.6C clear.
v1.0.21: T-18h 9pm. KPAE 11.1C, clouds building SCT070. Kp still at 3.00. KSC 21.8C clear.

v1.0.20: T-19h 8pm. CME EFFECTS BEGINNING — Kp surged 1.33→3.00, solar wind 406 km/s, Bz -2.6 nT southward. G2 watch holds for launch day. KPAE cooling 11.1C, CLR, S 170@8kt, pressure 1005.5 still falling.
v1.0.18: T-21h 6pm. KPAE 13.3C again — S wind shift 190@10kt. Pressure still falling (1006.5). FEW clouds. KSC cooling 22C. OPEN expanded to 16 problems with 4 new AI research cards.

SPACE WEATHER ALERT: X1.5 flare from AR4405 launched CME at ~1845 km/s. G2 Moderate watch for Apr 1 (LAUNCH DAY). Solar wind 387 km/s, Bz=-1. Kp forecast rising to 3.3. Crew radiation exposure being monitored.
v1.0.17: T-22h 5pm LIVE. KPAE cooling 12.2C from 13.3C peak. VRB light winds. Pressure dropping (1007.2, tend -2.4). NWS missed 5pm temp by 4.4C. KSC 24.5C clear, GO.
v1.0.16: T-23h 4pm. KPAE holding 13.3C peak. SW wind shift 230@8kt. Pressure 1007.9. KSC 24.5C clear.
v1.0.15: T-24h 3pm. KPAE warming to 13.3C afternoon peak. Kp up to 1.33. KSC 25.1C clear.
v1.0.14: T-25h 2pm. Peak 11.1C holding. Kp 0.67 very quiet. Pressure dropping. Radio guide expanded.
v1.0.13: T-26h 1pm. 11.1C peak. Variable wind. Kp dropped to 0.67.
Launch countdown begins at KSC
